subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

`Flip Angle`.

a single value for this field, or to the files in a

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

`Echo Time`

The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with

where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

issueMessage: Field description: Information describing the active/selected elements of the receiver coil.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: MatrixCoilMode
issueMessage: Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: CoilCombinationMethod
issueMessage: Field description: Almost all fMRI studies using phased-array coils use root-sum-of-squares
(rSOS) combination, but other methods exist.
The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: MRAcquisitionType
issueMessage: Field description: Type of sequence readout.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0023](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0023))
`MR Acquisition Type`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: FlipAngle
issueMessage: Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

subCode: InstitutionalDepartmentName
issueMessage: Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). Please note that derived (processed) data should be placed in /derivatives folder and source data (such as DICOMS or behavioural logs in proprietary formats) should be placed in the /sourcedata folder.
Empty files not allowed.

subCode: Resolution
issueMessage: Field description: Specifies the interpretation of the resolution keyword.
If an object is used, then the keys should be values for the `res` entity
and values should be descriptions of those `res` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the

subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

subCode: InstitutionalDepartmentName
issueMessage: Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). Please note that derived (processed) data should be placed in /derivatives folder and source data (such as DICOMS or behavioural logs in proprietary formats) should be placed in the /sourcedata folder.
Empty files not allowed.

subCode: Description
issueMessage: Field description: Free-form natural language description.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: SpatialReference
issueMessage: Field description: String or object describing the reference image to which the current image is aligned.
For images with a single reference, the value MUST be a single string.
For images with multiple references, such as surface and volume references,
a JSON object MUST be used.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: Resolution
issueMessage: Field description: Specifies the interpretation of the resolution keyword.
If an object is used, then the keys should be values for the `res` entity
and values should be descriptions of those `res` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

subCode: ReceiveCoilName
issueMessage: Field description: Information describing the receiver coil.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 1250](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1250))
`Receive Coil Name`,

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: ReceiveCoilActiveElements
issueMessage: Field description: Information describing the active/selected elements of the receiver coil.
This does not correspond to a tag in the DICOM ontology.
The vendor-defined terminology for active coil elements can go in this field.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: MatrixCoilMode
issueMessage: Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: CoilCombinationMethod
issueMessage: Field description: Almost all fMRI studies using phased-array coils use root-sum-of-squares
(rSOS) combination, but other methods exist.
The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: FlipAngle
issueMessage: Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

so anything non-standard should be reported.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.

issueMessage: Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.

by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). Please note that derived (processed) data should be placed in /derivatives folder and source data (such as DICOMS or behavioural logs in proprietary formats) should be placed in the /sourcedata folder.

issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

issueMessage: Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

subCode: CoilCombinationMethod

The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

subCode: RawSources

Field description: A list of paths relative to dataset root pointing to the BIDS-Raw file(s)
that were used in the creation of this derivative.
This field is DEPRECATED, and this metadata SHOULD be recorded in the
`Sources` field using [BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri)
to distinguish sources from different datasets.

For images with multiple references, such as surface and volume references,
a JSON object MUST be used.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is

`Echo Time`

The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a

where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

`MR Acquisition Type`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: FlipAngle
issueMessage: Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.

issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).

subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0087](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0087))
`Magnetic Field Strength`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: ReceiveCoilName
issueMessage: Field description: Information describing the receiver coil.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 1250](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1250))
`Receive Coil Name`,
although not all vendors populate that DICOM Tag,
in which case this field can be derived from an appropriate
private DICOM field.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: ReceiveCoilActiveElements
issueMessage: Field description: Information describing the active/selected elements of the receiver coil.
This does not correspond to a tag in the DICOM ontology.
The vendor-defined terminology for active coil elements can go in this field.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: MatrixCoilMode
issueMessage: Field description: (If used)
A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in
analog the signals from multiple coil elements.
There are typically different default modes when using un-accelerated or
accelerated (for example, `"GRAPPA"`, `"SENSE"`) imaging.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: CoilCombinationMethod
issueMessage: Field description: Almost all fMRI studies using phased-array coils use root-sum-of-squares
(rSOS) combination, but other methods exist.
The image reconstruction is changed by the coil combination method
(as for the matrix coil mode above),
so anything non-standard should be reported.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: MRAcquisitionType
issueMessage: Field description: Type of sequence readout.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0023](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0023))
`MR Acquisition Type`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: FlipAngle
issueMessage: Field description: Flip angle (FA) for the acquisition, specified in degrees.
Corresponds to: [DICOM Tag 0018, 1314](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,1314))
`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

subCode: InstitutionalDepartmentName
issueMessage: Field description: The department in the institution in charge of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). Please note that derived (processed) data should be placed in /derivatives folder and source data (such as DICOMS or behavioural logs in proprietary formats) should be placed in the /sourcedata folder.
Empty files not allowed.

subCode: Resolution
issueMessage: Field description: Specifies the interpretation of the resolution keyword.
If an object is used, then the keys should be values for the `res` entity
and values should be descriptions of those `res` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

subCode: Type
issueMessage: Field description: Short identifier of the mask.
The value `"Brain"` refers to a brain mask.
The value `"Lesion"` refers to a lesion mask.
The value `"Face"` refers to a face mask.
The value `"ROI"` refers to a region of interest mask.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: Sources
issueMessage: Field description: A list of files with the paths specified using
[BIDS URIs](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#bids-uri);
these files were directly used in the creation of this derivative data file.
For example, if a derivative A is used in the creation of another
derivative B, which is in turn used to generate C in a chain of A->B->C,
C should only list B in `"Sources"`, and B should only list A in `"Sources"`.
However, in case both X and Y are directly used in the creation of Z,
then Z should list X and Y in `"Sources"`,
regardless of whether X was used to generate Y.
Using paths specified relative to the dataset root is
[DEPRECATED](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#definitions).

A method for reducing the number of independent channels by combining in

so anything non-standard should be reported.

subCode: EchoTime
issueMessage: Field description: The echo time (TE) for the acquisition, specified in seconds.
Corresponds to [DICOM Tag 0018, 0081](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0081))
`Echo Time`
(please note that the DICOM term is in milliseconds not seconds).
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`echo` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#echo).

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: DwellTime
issueMessage: Field description: Actual dwell time (in seconds) of the receiver per point in the readout
direction, including any oversampling.
For Siemens, this corresponds to DICOM field 0019, 1018 (in ns).
This value is necessary for the optional readout distortion correction of
anatomicals in the HCP Pipelines.
It also usefully provides a handle on the readout bandwidth,
which isn't captured in the other metadata tags.
Not to be confused with `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"`, and the frequent mislabeling
of echo spacing (which is spacing in the phase encoding direction) as
"dwell time" (which is spacing in the readout direction).

`Flip Angle`.
The data type number may apply to files from any MRI modality concerned with
a single value for this field, or to the files in a
[file collection](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/file-collections.md)
where the value of this field is iterated using the
[`flip` entity](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/entities.md#flip).

subCode: PhaseEncodingDirection
issueMessage: Field description: The letters `i`, `j`, `k` correspond to the first, second and third axis of
the data in the NIFTI file.
The polarity of the phase encoding is assumed to go from zero index to
maximum index unless `-` sign is present
(then the order is reversed - starting from the highest index instead of
zero).
`PhaseEncodingDirection` is defined as the direction along which phase is was
modulated which may result in visible distortions.
Note that this is not the same as the DICOM term
`InPlanePhaseEncodingDirection` which can have `ROW` or `COL` values.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TotalReadoutTime
issueMessage: Field description: This is actually the "effective" total readout time,
defined as the readout duration, specified in seconds,
that would have generated data with the given level of distortion.
It is NOT the actual, physical duration of the readout train.
If `"EffectiveEchoSpacing"` has been properly computed,
it is just `EffectiveEchoSpacing * (ReconMatrixPE - 1)`.

subCode: SkullStripped
issueMessage: Field description: Whether the volume was skull stripped (non-brain voxels set to zero) or not.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: TaskName
issueMessage: Field description: Name of the task.
No two tasks should have the same name.
The task label included in the filename MAY be derived from this `"TaskName"` field
by removing all non-alphanumeric or `+` characters (that is, all except those matching `[0-9a-zA-Z+]`),
and potentially replacing spaces with `+` to ease readability.
For example `"TaskName"` `"faces n-back"` or `"head nodding"` could correspond to task labels
`faces+n+back` or `facesnback` and `head+nodding` or `headnodding`, respectively.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: RepetitionTime
issueMessage: Field description: The time in seconds between the beginning of an acquisition of one volume
and the beginning of acquisition of the volume following it (TR).
When used in the context of functional acquisitions this parameter best
corresponds to
[DICOM Tag 0020, 0110](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0020,0110)):
the "time delta between images in a
dynamic of functional set of images" but may also be found in
[DICOM Tag 0018, 0080](http://www.dicomlookup.com/dicomtags/(0018,0080)):
"the period of time in msec between the beginning
of a pulse sequence and the beginning of the succeeding
(essentially identical) pulse sequence".
This definition includes time between scans (when no data has been acquired)
in case of sparse acquisition schemes.
This value MUST be consistent with the 'pixdim[4]' field (after accounting
for units stored in 'xyzt_units' field) in the NIfTI header.
This field is mutually exclusive with VolumeTiming.

A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as required.
subCode: VolumeTiming
issueMessage: Field description: The time at which each volume was acquired during the acquisition.
It is described using a list of times referring to the onset of each volume
in the series.
The list must have the same length as the series,
and the values must be non-negative and monotonically increasing.
This field is mutually exclusive with `"RepetitionTime"`.

Files with such naming scheme are not part of BIDS specification. This error is most commonly caused by typos in file names that make them not BIDS compatible. Please consult the specification and make sure your files are named correctly. If this is not a file naming issue (for example when including files not yet covered by the BIDS specification) you should include a ".bidsignore" file in your dataset (see https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-validator#bidsignore for details). Please note that derived (processed) data should be placed in /derivatives folder and source data (such as DICOMS or behavioural logs in proprietary formats) should be placed in the /sourcedata folder.
